source.name: User curated premium video hero skill set
source.license_notes: User-curated prompt rules distilled into reusable local database guidance. Do not copy external media assets directly.

visual_traits: light and strong Liquid Glass hierarchy
layout_rules: Use flex row and min-h-screen over a full-screen video background.
Left panel carries main narrative and is around 52% width on desktop.
Right panel carries secondary information clusters and is around 48% width on desktop.
Hide the right panel on mobile and prioritize the left main narrative.
Left panel contains brand navigation, logo, large headline, CTA, tags, pills, quote, or brand statement.
Right panel can contain social icon pill, account/sparkle button, community card, feature preview cards, bottom feature card, thumbnail, or plus button.
Information clusters must support the main story and must not feel like random floating decoration.
interaction_states: Glass CTA hover uses scale-105; active uses scale-95.
Light glass supports pills, chips, social buttons, small cards, secondary controls, and feature cards.
Strong glass supports CTA, large panels, main overlays, primary cards, and major information blocks.
Do not use the same glass intensity everywhere.
Use video overlays or reduced opacity to preserve text and CTA readability.
Mobile should remove or collapse secondary clusters instead of cramming them under the headline.
accessibility_notes: Keep primary narrative first in DOM order.
Hide decorative right-panel elements from assistive tech if they do not add meaning.
Ensure mobile retains CTA and main value proposition.
Check contrast on every glass cluster over video.
Do not rely on glass blur alone for text readability.
Respect reduced motion and reduced transparency.
implementation_hints: Desktop layout: left panel about 52%, right panel about 48%.
Use .liquid-glass for light elements and .liquid-glass-strong for CTA or major panels.
Premium glass boundaries use ::before, padding 1px to 1.5px, linear-gradient rim, and mask-composite exclude/xor where supported.
Glass UI needs hierarchy: light glass is supporting; strong glass is closer to the viewer.
Use directional overlays when video brightness changes behind either panel.
avoid_when: right cluster unrelated to hero story
same glass intensity on every element
forcing desktop right panel onto small screens

quality.notes: Directly distilled from the user-curated two-panel Liquid Glass split hero rules.

retrieval.ai_summary: Two-panel Liquid Glass split hero for premium creative AI, art, design, lifestyle, and visual-generation landing pages. Uses full-screen video, left narrative around 52%, right information cluster around 48%, right side hidden on mobile, two-tier glass hierarchy, strong glass CTA, light glass pills/cards, and pseudo-element gradient rims.
